Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY NICHOLS VS. MARK STREHLOW ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Mark Strehlows Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents Of Truevalue Labs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MARK STREHLOW'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Personal Appearance And Production Of Documents Of Truevalue Labs. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Subject to the representations of Defendant that all wage and income claims are withdrawn, Plaintiff has not met the threshold necessary to require disclosure of private financial information. The denial of this motion is without prejudice should Plaintiff make a sufficient showing that income/earnings of Defendant are relevant to Plaintiff's or Defendant's claims. The deposition subpoena and request for documents shall be amended to delete reference to Defendant's earnings information. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to JSKrmpotic@lowball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL T JENKS VS. 346 FIRST STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON PROFIT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 346 First Street Owners' Association Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories 36-49 And 52-54 And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,275
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 1, Defendant 346 First Street Owners' Association'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories 36-49 And 52-54 And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $10,275. Pro Tem Judge Stephanie Krmpotic, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted as to those interrogatories where plaintiff has incorporated by reference the FAC and the 75 page response to interrogatory 36. The response is not sufficiently specific and straightforward in identifying documents and witnesses requested. (CCP 2030.220(a)) Further responses are required which do not incorporate the entirety of the response to interrogatory 36 and FAC. Plaintiff is to provide a further response to interrogatories 36, 39, 46, 51, 52 identifying documents or categories of documents in a complete and straightforward manner. Plaintiff is to provide a further response to interrogatory 38 identifying witnesses in a straightforward manner. Plaintiff is to provide a further response to interrogatories 40, 45, and 54 to provide a straightforward response. Further responses are due within 30-days. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to JSKrmpotic@lowball.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY KLEIN VS. NEWELL & COMPANY, INC, DBA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 8, CROSS-DEFENDANT PETER QUIRKE'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. Cross-defendant Peter Quirke's motion for determination of good faith of his settlement with plaintiff Jeffrey Klein is denied without prejudice to being refiled based on a better evidentiary record. Mr. Quirke again fails to present substantial evidence showing a rough approximation of plaintiff's recovery and Mr. Quirke's proportionate liability at the time of the settlement in September of 2016. The addition of the declarations of Mr. Price and Mr. Stokes do not cure the evidentiary gap that existed in the prior good faith motion. At most, they show that Mr. Klein incurred $342,424 in repair costs. There is insufficient information regarding a total approximation of Mr. Klein's damages, including consequential damages such as loss of use damages, that existed at the time of the settlement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Newell & Co. is required to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DON TUCKER ET AL VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 12, PLAINTIFFS DON TUCKER AND MARTIN AGUILAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues. Plaintiff Don Tucker's motion for summary adjudication against defendant Travelers Indemnity Company of Connecticut on the issues of whether Travelers owed a duty to defend Mr. Tucker in the subrogation and underlying personal injury actions is granted. Mr. Tucker has shown that there is a potential for coverage in the CGL policy issued by Travelers to Mr. Tucker for Martin Aguilar's claims against Mr. Tucker. The residential exclusion does not relieve Travelers of a duty to defend because the language of that exclusion, narrowly and strictly construed as required by California law, only excludes construction work done for a structure designed for housing, which a swimming pool is not. Even assuming that Travelers' proffered interpretation of the residential exclusion provision is as reasonable as Mr. Tucker's proffered interpretation, Travelers still owed a duty to defend to Mr. Tucker. When there are two reasonable interpretations of an exclusion, the interpretation favoring the insured controls. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Tucker is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DON TUCKER ET AL VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY OF CONNECTICUT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Diane K. Fraziers Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 12, Defendant Diane K. Frazier's Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment. Defendant Diane Frazier's motion for summary judgment on the sole cause of action for negligent misrepresentation alleged against her in the complaint filed by plaintiff Don Tucker is denied. Per California law, an insured has a claim against an adjuster who is employed by an insurer based on a misrepresentation made by the adjuster. Ms. Frazier's asserted statement that "there is no coverage for this loss, as the work was taking place at a residence in San Francisco and there is a full residential exclusion on that policy" is an actionable representation of fact because it is a provably false statement. There is a triable dispute whether Ms. Frazier's statement was false because the excavation of the swimming pool is not encompassed within the residential exclusion. Mr. Tucker's objections to the evidence submitted by Ms. Frazier with her reply papers are sustained. There are also triable disputes whether 1) Ms. Frazier had reasonable grounds for believing her statement was true; 2) Ms. Frazier intended for Mr. Tucker to rely on her statement; 3) Mr. Tucker actually and reasonably relied on the statement; and 4) Mr. Tucker suffered harm as a result of his reliance on the statement. Liberally construing the evidence in favor of Mr. Tucker and drawing all inferences in his favor, there is evidence showing: a) Ms. Frazier made the statement within hours of receiving a claim without review and approval of her supervisor contrary to Travelers' policies; b) Ms. Frazier knew her statement would be communicated to Mr. Tucker and would be relied on by him in his business decisions; and c) Mr. Tucker purchased additional insurance because of the statement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Mr. Tucker is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EVANS ET AL VS. DEALYZE INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 13, DEFENDANTS DEALYZE INC., A FLORIDA CORPORATION, DEALYZE INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' motion to strike the second amended complaint and request for sanctions is denied. The motion should not have been filed and is an unfortunate example of why civil litigation has become so expensive and fractious. Any concern or uncertainty by defendants' counsel whether plaintiffs intended to proceed with a second amended complaint - that had been timely served but, through excusable mistake, not timely filed - could and should have been resolved by a simple inquiry to plaintiffs' counsel. The aspersions cast on plaintiffs and their counsel in the moving and reply papers are unwarranted. Plaintiffs' counsel has shown admirable restraint by not seeking sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: III-INTERACTIVE LLC DBA DIVISION D VS. ZEPHYER DIGITAL INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Verification Of Discovery Response, Production Of Documents, Supplemental Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 14, PLAINTIFF III-INTERACTIVE LLC DBA DIVISION D'S Notice Of Motion To Compel Verification Of Discovery Response, Production Of Documents, Supplemental Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Admissions. Plaintiff III-Interactive, LLC's motion to compel verification of discovery response, production of documents, supplemental responses to interrogatories and requests for admissions is off calendar as moot and its requests for monetary sanctions is denied. Per the report of Judge Pro Tem David McDonald, defendant Zephyr Digital, Inc. has now provided all the discovery that was sought to be compelled. The court adopts in full the recommendation and reasons for that recommendation in the report of Judge Pro Tem David McDonald as to plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and attaches the report of Judge Pro Tem David McDonald and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GENERATION NAILS A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP VS. SENTINEL INSURANCE COMPANY LIMITED, A CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
rulings.Rulings: The matter is on calendar for Tuesday, July 17, 2018, Line 15, PLAINTIFFS GENERATION NAILS A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP, GENERATION NAILS LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, THUONG HOANG, ANH NGUYEN'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. Plaintiffs Generation Nails, Generation Nails, LLC, Thuong Hoang and Anh Nguyen's motion for stay of proceedings is granted in part. Effective immediately all proceedings in this case, including all discovery, are stayed until the conclusion of the underlying case with the sole exception that defendant Sentinel Insurance Company may file and have heard a summary motion limited to the issue of whether it owes a duty to defend. Plaintiffs may oppose the motion on any grounds they see fit, including that resolution of the motion requires determination of facts that overlap with facts that are in dispute in the underlying action and thus the court should refrain from ruling on the motion until after the conclusion of the underlying case. The only basis for opposition of this stay motion is Sentinel's contention that it should not have to wait till the conclusion of the underlying action to establish that it does not owe a duty to defend and that it can do so without need for determination of any facts that overlap with facts that are in dispute in the underlying action. Sentinel's position appears to be correct, but it may turn out that a summary motion filed by it shows that its position is incorrect. Balancing fairness to both sides and fidelity to case law favors the limited exception to the stay provided for in this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Sentinel is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
